bio.quote: Thráinn Ashward is the commanding Inquisitor of the Pale Maw forces at Beinvaka. Tall and gaunt with a scar across one eye, he wears robes of deep purple with silver entropy symbols. His manner is cli

system.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

system.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

novice.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

system.description: Triggered action to take half damage from non-untyped damage (fire, cold, etc.).

novice.description: Triggered action to take half damage from non-untyped damage (fire, cold, etc.).

system.description: Advantage (Second Chance) on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.

novice.description: Advantage (Second Chance) on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.

system.description:
Nomad lines that winter beside rib-cairns and bone-fields, keeping the old fires and the old taboos while carrying authority by rite and remembrance.

Language: Beinmarksmål

Environment: Nomadic; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Noble

system.description:
Skills: Cunning/Lore, Vigilant, Cunning.

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 150

Perk: *True Omen*. Once per session, after you study signs or consult a rite, gain an advantage (second chance) on one test to interpret a supernatural or symbolic clue.

novice.description:
Skills: Cunning/Lore, Vigilant, Cunning.

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 150

system.description: Whenever you fail a test to recall lore using a skill from the lore skill group, you instinctively recall the nearest location where the information you seek might be found. This could be the tower of a local sage, a library in a nearby city, somewhere deep in a dungeon, or any other location of the Director’s determination. The Director can decide that certain lore can’t be revealed this way.

novice.description: Whenever you fail a test to recall lore using a skill from the lore skill group, you instinctively recall the nearest location where the information you seek might be found. This could be the tower of a local sage, a library in a nearby city, somewhere deep in a dungeon, or any other location of the Director’s determination. The Director can decide that certain lore can’t be revealed this way.

system.description:
Demons and deathless fear you. Criminals run from the sight of your shadow. Agents of chaos, blasphemers, and heretics tremble at the sound of your voice. You carry the power of the gods, armed with wrath and sent out into the world first to seek, then censor those whose actions—or even existence—are anathema to your church. 

As a censor, you’re at your best against the strongest foes. Your judgment terrifies heretics, stops enemies in their tracks, and even hurls them across the battlefield.

novice.description:
Demons and deathless fear you. Criminals run from the sight of your shadow. Agents of chaos, blasphemers, and heretics tremble at the sound of your voice. You carry the power of the gods, armed with wrath and sent out into the world first to seek, then censor those whose actions—or even existence—are anathema to your church. 

As a censor, you’re at your best against the strongest foes. Your judgment terrifies heretics, stops enemies in their tracks, and even hurls them across the battlefield.

system.description: Without a strong example and a firm hand, the weak will be corrupted. You specialize in setting an example for your order. You have the Lead skill.

novice.description: Without a strong example and a firm hand, the weak will be corrupted. You specialize in setting an example for your order. You have the Lead skill.

system.description: The first time on a turn that you use your @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.EhPpL42VZWxqeSEj]{Judgement} ability to judge a creature, you can vertical pull the judged creature up to a number of meters equal to [[lookup 2*@P evaluate=true]]{twice your Presence score}.

novice.description: The first time on a turn that you use your @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.EhPpL42VZWxqeSEj]{Judgement} ability to judge a creature, you can vertical pull the judged creature up to a number of meters equal to [[lookup 2*@P evaluate=true]]{twice your Presence score}.

system.description: Your devotion to your deity allows you to take command of the battlefield, letting your allies benefit from your wisdom. While you are adjacent to a creature, your allies gain the benefits of flanking against that creature. Additionally, your allies gain an advantage (second chance) on tests made to aid other creatures with their tests.

novice.description: Your devotion to your deity allows you to take command of the battlefield, letting your allies benefit from your wisdom. While you are adjacent to a creature, your allies gain the benefits of flanking against that creature. Additionally, your allies gain an advantage (second chance) on tests made to aid other creatures with their tests.

system.description: You exhibit a small spark of your deity’s power, causing creatures to trust or fear you, depending on what you need. You gain an advantage (second chance) on tests made to intimidate or persuade others.

novice.description: You exhibit a small spark of your deity’s power, causing creatures to trust or fear you, depending on what you need. You gain an advantage (second chance) on tests made to intimidate or persuade others.

system.description: Your divine spark grows in power, allowing you and your allies to focus and endure. At the start of each of your turns, you can spend 1d6 Toughness to end one effect on you that is ended by a attribute test or that ends at the end of your turn. Any ally who starts their turn within 5 meters of you can also spend Toughness to gain this benefit.

novice.description: Your divine spark grows in power, allowing you and your allies to focus and endure. At the start of each of your turns, you can spend 1d6 Toughness to end one effect on you that is ended by a attribute test or that ends at the end of your turn. Any ally who starts their turn within 5 meters of you can also spend Toughness to gain this benefit.

system.description: When you use your Hands of the Maker ability, you can create a mundane object that is size 2 or smaller.

novice.description: When you use your Hands of the Maker ability, you can create a mundane object that is size 2 or smaller.

system.description: The Spellsword kit combines melee strikes and a little bit of magic, letting you create a warrior who doesn’t have to choose between the incantation and the blade.

novice.description: The Spellsword kit combines melee strikes and a little bit of magic, letting you create a warrior who doesn’t have to choose between the incantation and the blade.

items.name: Look On My Work and Despair

system.description: Your judgment has grown in divine power, instilling fear in those you condemn. Whenever you use your Judgment ability, you can spend 1 wrath, and if the target has [[potency P average]], they are frightened of you (save ends). Additionally, whenever a creature judged by you is reduced to 0 Toughness and you use Judgment as a free triggered action, if the new target has [[potency P strong]], they are frightened of you (save ends). If the target is already frightened of you, they instead take {holy damage} equal to twice your Presence score.

novice.description: Your judgment has grown in divine power, instilling fear in those you condemn. Whenever you use your Judgment ability, you can spend 1 wrath, and if the target has [[potency P average]], they are frightened of you (save ends). Additionally, whenever a creature judged by you is reduced to 0 Toughness and you use Judgment as a free triggered action, if the new target has [[potency P strong]], they are frightened of you (save ends). If the target is already frightened of you, they instead take {holy damage} equal to twice your Presence score.

system.description: Whenever you make a test that uses the Brag, Flirt, or Intimidate skills, you can use Might instead of any other characteristic the test calls for.

novice.description: Whenever you make a test that uses the Brag, Flirt, or Intimidate skills, you can use Might instead of any other characteristic the test calls for.

system.description: On any day when you don’t take a extended rest, you can spend 1 uninterrupted hour working on a crafting project using a skill you have from the crafting skill group. If you do so, you gain project points toward that project.

novice.description: On any day when you don’t take a extended rest, you can spend 1 uninterrupted hour working on a crafting project using a skill you have from the crafting skill group. If you do so, you gain project points toward that project.

system.description:
The power you serve grants you a Heroic Resource called wrath, fueling your abilities as you censor those your church deems to be heretics.

Wrath in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain wrath equal to [[lookup @hero.milestones]]{your Milestones}. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you {gain 2 wrath}.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that a creature judged by you (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.EhPpL42VZWxqeSEj]{Judgement}) deals damage to you, you {gain 1 wrath}. The first time each combat round that you deal damage to a creature judged by you, you {gain 1 wrath}.

You lose any remaining wrath at the end of the encounter.

Wrath Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain wrath outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost wrath without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs wrath, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited wrath on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of wrath equal to [[lookup @hero.milestones]]{your Milestones}. (Such abilities aren’t part of the core rules for the censor, but they might appear in future products.)

system.description: The first time each combat round that you deal damage to a creature judged by you, you gain 2 wrath instead of 1.

novice.description: The first time each combat round that you deal damage to a creature judged by you, you gain 2 wrath instead of 1.
